analysis_section.description: Compute one weekly analysis section only. Use this for asynchronous section caches; it does not build the full weekly analysis report.

program.description: Optional program snapshot supplied by the caller

refresh_program.description: Invalidate the program cache before analysis

section.description: The individual analysis section to compute

sessions.description: Optional session snapshot supplied by the caller

week_end.description: Inclusive training week number to end analysis

week_start.description: Inclusive training week number to start analysis

window_end.description: Optional date window end (YYYY-MM-DD) for time-series context

window_start.description: Optional date window start (YYYY-MM-DD) for time-series context

analyze_powerlifting_stats.description: Compares a user's powerlifting stats against the OpenPowerlifting dataset.

analyze_progression.description: Calculate weekly progression rate (kg/week) for a lift via Theil-Sen regression on top sets. Returns slope, Kendall tau, fit quality, and data points.

weeks.description: Number of recent weeks to analyze

analyze_rpe_drift.description: Detect RPE drift for a lift — whether perceived exertion is trending up (fatigue) or down (adaptation).

block_correlation_analysis.description: AI-powered exercise ROI correlation analysis for a supplied block snapshot. Use this for past blocks so the correlation uses that block's sessions rather than today's rolling window.

block_program_evaluation.description: AI program evaluation for a supplied historical block snapshot. The caller supplies a normalized program where the target block sessions are scoped to current. Use this for past-block program analysis without loading the live current program.

budget_advisor.description: AI budget triage and pre-competition priority analysis for a powerlifter. Returns a structured assessment: overall status vs monthly cap, locked-in MANDATORY items, suggested cuts when over budget, missing-expense gaps based on the nearest competition, and a coach-facing note. Competition day is the north star — mandatory comp-linked items are never suggested for cuts.

competitions.description: Optional upcoming competitions with name, start_date, user_status.

items.description: Budget items with id, name, category, cost, recurrence, priority_tier, purchased, comp linkage.

spent_this_month.description: Total spent in the current month, for over/under-cap reasoning.

budget_priority_timeline.description: AI purchase priority timeline for a powerlifting budget. Schedules equipment, supplements, gym/federation memberships, and competition-entry fees across months so the total stays under the monthly cap while ensuring mandatory items are bought before each confirmed/optional competition.

competitions.description: Optional upcoming competitions with master_id, name, start_date, user_status.

federation_memberships.description: Optional federation membership state: abbreviation, membership_group, membership_paid, membership_cost.

items.description: Budget items (equipment, supplements, memberships, competition entries).

calculate_attempts.description: Calculate competition attempt weights based on opener.

last_felt.description: If 'hard', halve j2 for conservative third attempt

lift.description: Lift type: squat, bench, or deadlift

calculate_dots.description: Calculate DOTS score from competition total and bodyweight.

correlation_analysis.description: AI-powered exercise ROI correlation analysis. Identifies which accessory exercises correlate with improvements in squat/bench/deadlift over a rolling window. Results are cached in DynamoDB. Use refresh=true to force regeneration.

cache_only.description: Return only cached results without generating AI output

days_until.description: Calculate days until a target date.

estimate_1rm.description: Estimate one-rep max using Epley, Brzycki, and RPE-based formulas.

export_program_history.description: Export the full training program to an Excel (.xlsx) or Markdown (.md) file. Sheets: Meta, Current Maxes, Phases, Sessions, Exercises, Competitions, Lift Profiles, Weekly Analysis, Per-Lift Metrics, ROI Correlation, Program Evaluation. The three AI-driven sheets use cached values; refresh on the Analysis page first if needed. After calling, emit a FILES: line to deliver the file.

export_program_markdown.description: Generate the markdown export of the current program and return its content as a string. Used internally by regenerate_analysis.

fatigue_profile_estimate.description: Estimate the fatigue profile (axial/neural/peripheral/systemic components) for an exercise using AI analysis of biomechanical characteristics.

get_analysis_markdown.description: Return the cached markdown export of the full training program (current block). This is the primary reference document for coaching decisions. Use the cache unless it is stale, dirty, or refresh is true.

glossary_estimate_e1rm.description: AI backfill e1RM estimate for one exercise.

glossary_estimate_fatigue.description: AI fatigue profile estimation for one exercise.

glossary_estimate_muscles.description: AI muscle group estimation for one glossary exercise.

glossary_generate_text.description: Generate concise editable glossary text for what an exercise is, how to perform it, and why to use it.

glossary_set_e1rm.description: Manually set e1RM for glossary exercise.

health_add_exercise.description: Add an exercise to a training session.

exercise.description: Exercise dict: {name, sets, reps, kg, rpe, notes}

health_complete_competition.description: Mark a competition as completed and compute PRR from the stored snapshot.

allow_retrospective.description: Allow backfilling a missing T-1 snapshot

post_meet_report.description: Optional structured post-meet attempt and context report

results.description: Best successful lift attempts and total

competition.description: Competition dict: name, date, federation, federation_id, counts_toward_federation_ids, status, weight_class_kg, location, targets, notes

health_delete_competition.description: Delete a competition entry by date.

health_delete_diet_note.description: Delete a diet note by date.

health_delete_session.description: Delete a training session by date.

health_get_current_maxes.description: Get current training maxes (squat, bench, deadlift).

health_get_diet_notes.description: Get diet notes for a date range.

health_get_federation_library.description: Get the shared federation and qualification-standards library.

health_get_goals.description: Get the explicit goals for the current training block.

health_get_meta.description: Get program metadata (name, dates, weight class, etc.).

health_get_phases.description: Get the training phases from the program.

health_get_program.description: Get the full training program from DynamoDB. Returns the cached program dict with all sessions, phases, meta, and preferences.

health_get_session.description: Get a single training session by date.

health_get_sessions_range.description: Get training sessions for a date range.

health_get_supplements.description: Get the supplement protocol from the program.

health_invalidate_program_cache.description: Clear the in-memory cached training program so the next read loads from DynamoDB.

health_new_version.description: Create a new program version with the given patches.

change_reason.description: Human-readable reason for the version change

patches.description: List of patches, each with 'path' and 'value' keys

health_remove_exercise.description: Remove an exercise from a training session by index.

exercise_index.description: Zero-based index of the exercise to remove

health_reschedule_session.description: Move a training session from one date to another.

health_setup_initialize.description: Initialize the first valid current training block for a no-data user.

maxes.description: Optional maxes/e1RMs keyed by squat, bench, deadlift, or template glossary IDs

health_setup_status.description: Return no-data onboarding setup state for the active training data partition.

health_snapshot_competition_projection.description: Snapshot projected maxes 7 days before a competition and optionally backfill missed snapshots.

patch.description: Fields to update (targets, status, notes, etc.)

health_update_diet_note.description: Create or replace a diet note for a date.

health_update_goals.description: Replace the explicit goals array for the current training block.

goals.description: Complete goals array to write to the current block

health_update_meta.description: Update program metadata fields, including sex for DOTS calculations.

updates.description: Dict of meta fields to update

phases.description: Complete phases list. Each: {name, start_week, end_week, intent}

health_update_session.description: Update fields on an existing training session.

date.description: ISO8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) of the session to update

patch.description: Dict with session fields to update. Allowed keys: completed, session_rpe, body_weight_kg, session_notes, exercises

import_apply.description: Apply a staged import to the program or template library.

import_get_pending.description: Get a single pending import by ID.

import_parse_file.description: Parse a spreadsheet file and stage it as a pending import.

ipf_weight_classes.description: Get IPF weight classes for a given sex.

lift_profile_estimate_stimulus.description: Estimate a 1-2 INOL stimulus coefficient from an existing lift profile. Requires profile completeness score >= 55.

profile.description: Lift profile with lift, style_notes, sticking_points, primary_muscle, and volume_tolerance.

lift_profile_review.description: Review a squat, bench, or deadlift style profile and return missing biomechanical details needed to estimate a lift-specific INOL stimulus coefficient.

profile.description: Lift profile with lift, style_notes, sticking_points, primary_muscle, and volume_tolerance.

lift_profile_rewrite.description: Rewrite lift profile text for analysis clarity without estimating stimulus coefficient.

profile.description: Lift profile with lift, style_notes, sticking_points, primary_muscle, and volume_tolerance.

lift_profile_rewrite_and_estimate.description: Rewrite a lift style profile for analysis clarity and estimate a 1-2 INOL stimulus coefficient against a baseline of 1.0.

profile.description: Lift profile with lift, style_notes, sticking_points, primary_muscle, and volume_tolerance.

multi_block_comparison_analysis.description: AI comparison of current and historical block analysis bundles. Identifies similarities, differences, lift-specific outcomes, ROI, volume dose response, bodyweight/training-day relationships, projection accuracy, fatigue patterns, and best-value blocks.

payload.description: Multi-block comparison payload built from block analytics.

muscle_group_estimate.description: Estimate the primary, secondary, and tertiary muscle groups for an exercise using AI analysis of the movement and the user's lift profiles.

lift_profiles.description: Optional squat/bench/deadlift lift profiles to pass through to the estimator

pct_of_max.description: Calculate a percentage of a max weight.

powerlifting_filter_categories.description: Retrieves the unique options available for filtering the OpenPowerlifting dataset.

powerlifting_ranking_percentile.description: Returns national/regional/global top-percentile cards for the dashboard. Filters the OpenPowerlifting dataset to the 3 nearest IPF weight classes, last 3 calendar years, deduplicated by lifter (best total per name). Returns percentile (0-100) for Squat/Bench/Deadlift/Total across global, national (country), and regional (country+region) scopes. A value is null when <10 comparison lifters or the lift was not provided.

country.description: Filter national scope (MeetCountry value from dataset)

region.description: Filter regional scope (State value from dataset)

program_evaluation.description: Full-block AI program evaluation by a sports scientist. Evaluates the current block against competition goals, identifies what is working and not, and suggests small targeted changes. Requires >= 4 completed weeks. Cached weekly. Use refresh=true to force regeneration.

cache_only.description: Return only cached results without generating AI output

regenerate_analysis.description: Regenerate deterministic current-block analysis caches: 6 weekly windows and the markdown export. Call this when the operator asks to refresh or regenerate their training analysis. This intentionally does not regenerate AI correlation reports, AI program evaluation, past-block caches, or the lifetime compare AI cache.

template_apply.description: Apply a template to the program block (preview).

actor_pk.description: Signed-in user/template author partition for draft visibility

template_apply_confirm.description: Confirm and write concretized block from template.

actor_pk.description: Signed-in user/template author partition for draft visibility

template_create_blank.description: Create a new blank training template with no sessions.

template_create_from_payload.description: Create a complete reusable training template atomically from a structured payload.

actor_pk.description: Signed-in user/template author partition for draft visibility
Signed-in user/template author partition for draft visibility
Signed-in user/template author partition for draft visibility

template_publish.description: Publish an authored draft template so everyone can see and apply it.

template_unpublish.description: Unpublish an authored template so only its author can see it.

template_update.description: Overwrite an existing training template in place (metadata, phases, sessions).

weekly_analysis.description: Full weekly training analysis — progression, RPE drift, fatigue index, compliance, meet projection. Returns structured JSON.

program.description: Optional program snapshot supplied by the caller

refresh_program.description: Invalidate the program cache before analysis

sessions.description: Optional session snapshot supplied by the caller

week_end.description: Inclusive training week number to end analysis

week_start.description: Inclusive training week number to start analysis

window_end.description: Optional date window end (YYYY-MM-DD) for time-series context

window_start.description: Optional date window start (YYYY-MM-DD) for time-series context
